---
title: "Grain Detection Parameter Comparison: 20260408_C2600-06tx200_s"
date: today
author: "yoshinobu.ishizaki"
format:
  gfm:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(jsonlite)
library(fitdistrplus)
library(knitr)
library(gt)

# ---- Condition definitions ----
conditions <- tibble(
  key   = c("moderate_threshold", "opt_threshold", "default_color", "opt_color"),
  label = c("Moderate Threshold", "Optimised Threshold", "Default Color Region", "Optimised Color Region"),
  method = c("threshold", "threshold", "color_region", "color_region")
)

cond_colours <- c(
  "Moderate Threshold"     = "#1f78b4",
  "Optimised Threshold"    = "#33a02c",
  "Default Color Region"   = "#e31a1c",
  "Optimised Color Region" = "#ff7f00"
)

# ---- Load grain CSVs ----
grain_col_types <- cols(
  grain_id                    = col_character(),
  area_pixels                 = col_double(),
  equivalent_diameter_pixels  = col_double(),
  centroid_x                  = col_double(),
  centroid_y                  = col_double(),
  eccentricity                = col_double(),
  solidity                    = col_double(),
  area_um2                    = col_double(),
  equivalent_diameter_um      = col_double()
)

load_grain_csv <- function(key, label) {
  f <- paste0("cmp_", key, "_grain.csv")
  if (!file.exists(f)) stop(paste("Missing:", f))
  read_csv(f, col_types = grain_col_types) |>
    mutate(condition = factor(label, levels = conditions$label))
}

grains_list <- map2(conditions$key, conditions$label, load_grain_csv)
grains <- bind_rows(grains_list)

# ---- Load JSON params for each condition ----
load_param_json <- function(key) {
  f <- paste0("param_cmp_", key, ".json")
  if (!file.exists(f)) return(list())
  fromJSON(f)
}

params_list <- setNames(map(conditions$key, load_param_json), conditions$key)

# ---- Descriptive statistics ----
stats_tbl <- grains |>
  group_by(condition) |>
  summarise(
    N               = n(),
    mean_diam_um    = mean(equivalent_diameter_um),
    median_diam_um  = median(equivalent_diameter_um),
    sd_diam_um      = sd(equivalent_diameter_um),
    cv_pct          = 100 * sd(equivalent_diameter_um) / mean(equivalent_diameter_um),
    min_diam_um     = min(equivalent_diameter_um),
    max_diam_um     = max(equivalent_diameter_um),
    mean_area_um2   = mean(area_um2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

grains_with_data <- grains

# ---- Log-normal fitting (only conditions with data) ----
fits <- map(conditions$label, function(lbl) {
  x <- grains_with_data |> filter(condition == lbl) |> pull(equivalent_diameter_um)
  tryCatch(fitdist(x, "lnorm"), error = function(e) NULL)
})
names(fits) <- conditions$label

extract_lnorm_info <- function(fits) {
  map_dfr(names(fits), function(lbl) {
    f <- fits[[lbl]]
    if (is.null(f)) return(tibble(condition = lbl, meanlog = NA_real_, sdlog = NA_real_, AIC = NA_real_, BIC = NA_real_))
    p <- f$estimate
    tibble(
      condition = lbl,
      meanlog   = round(p["meanlog"], 4),
      sdlog     = round(p["sdlog"],   4),
      AIC       = round(f$aic, 1),
      BIC       = round(f$bic, 1)
    )
  })
}

fit_info <- extract_lnorm_info(fits)

# Condition with lowest AIC (best fit overall)
best_cond <- fit_info |> slice_min(AIC, n = 1) |> pull(condition)
```

## Overview

This document compares three grain-detection parameter configurations applied to the same
brass microstructure image (`20260408_C2600-06tx200_s.jpg`, C2600 un-annealed, ×200).
A fourth condition (Optimised Threshold) is planned for a future update pending optimizer completion.

| # | Condition | Detection method |
|---|-----------|-----------------|
| 1 | Moderate Threshold | GSAT pipeline — adaptive threshold, CLAHE clip=2, denoise_h=0.1, invert=true |
| 2 | Optimised Threshold | GSAT pipeline — auto-optimised via random search (seed 42, starting from moderate params) |
| 3 | Default Color Region | Felzenszwalb — scale = 200, sigma = 0.8, min_size = 100 |
| 4 | Optimised Color Region | Felzenszwalb — auto-optimised via random search (seed 42) |

All conditions share the same image ROI (`x=0, y=191, w=1439, h=603`),
scale factor (`pixels_per_um = 0.975`), and grain-filtering settings
(`min_grain_area = 50 px²`, `exclude_edge_grains = true`).

---

## Conclusion

Visually, **Default Color Region** (Felzenszwalb, scale=200, sigma=0.8, min_size=100)
produced the most convincing grain boundary detection for this image.
The 2064 detected grains align well with the visible microstructure, and the overlay
shows clean, continuous boundaries that closely follow the actual grain outlines.

Key findings:

- The **threshold pipeline** requires non-default settings to work at all on this image
  (`invert_grayscale=true`, gentle denoising, adaptive thresholding). Even with moderate
  tuning it detects fewer grains (1149) than the color-region methods.
  The optimiser converged to only 176 grains — evidence that the threshold approach
  struggles with the low boundary-to-grain contrast of this brass micrograph.
- The **color-region pipeline** (Felzenszwalb) works well out-of-the-box. The default
  settings yielded the highest grain count (2064) and visually the best-looking overlay.
  Optimisation shifted the parameters toward a coarser segmentation (scale=100, sigma=0.3)
  resulting in 1470 grains with a slightly larger mean diameter.
- **Log-normal distribution** provided a good fit for all conditions (AIC minimum =
  **`r best_cond`**), consistent with the expected grain-size distribution in polycrystalline metals.

**Recommendation**: Use Default Color Region as the starting point for this sample type.
Apply the optimiser only if the default segmentation visually over- or under-segments
the boundaries for a given image.

---

## Preparation

The CSV data and overlay images below were generated with the following commands
(run from repo root):

```bash
# Run all from tests/sample/param_comp/

# Condition 1: moderate threshold
uv run ../../../src/grainsize_measure_cli.py param_cmp_moderate_threshold.json \
  --out grain chord stat image --oname cmp_moderate_threshold

# Condition 2: optimised threshold
uv run ../../../scripts/optimize_params.py \
  --params param_cmp_moderate_threshold.json \
  --out param_cmp_opt_threshold.json --seed 42
uv run ../../../src/grainsize_measure_cli.py param_cmp_opt_threshold.json \
  --out grain chord stat image --oname cmp_opt_threshold

# Condition 3: default color region
uv run ../../../src/grainsize_measure_cli.py param_cmp_default_color.json \
  --out grain chord stat image --oname cmp_default_color

# Condition 4: optimised color region
uv run ../../../scripts/optimize_params.py \
  --params param_cmp_default_color.json \
  --out param_cmp_opt_color.json --seed 42
uv run ../../../src/grainsize_measure_cli.py param_cmp_opt_color.json \
  --out grain chord stat image --oname cmp_opt_color
```

---

## Original Image

![Original microstructure](20260408_C2600-06tx200_s.jpg){width=700}

---

## Overlay Image Comparison

| Moderate Threshold | Optimised Threshold |
|:---:|:---:|
| ![](cmp_moderate_threshold_overlay.png){width=350} | ![](cmp_opt_threshold_overlay.png){width=350} |

| Default Color Region | Optimised Color Region |
|:---:|:---:|
| ![](cmp_default_color_overlay.png){width=350} | ![](cmp_opt_color_overlay.png){width=350} |

---

## Analysis Parameters

```{r params-table}
# Parameters that only apply to each detection method
threshold_only_keys <- c(
  "threshold_method", "threshold_value", "threshold_high",
  "adaptive_block_size", "clahe_clip_limit",
  "denoise_h", "sharpen_amount",
  "morph_close_radius", "morph_open_radius", "min_feature_size"
)
color_only_keys <- c(
  "color_scale", "color_sigma", "color_min_size", "color_morph_close_radius"
)

get_param <- function(plist, key) {
  method <- plist[["detection_method"]]
  # Return "—" for parameters that don't apply to this detection method
  if (key %in% threshold_only_keys && !is.null(method) && method == "color_region") return("—")
  if (key %in% color_only_keys     && !is.null(method) && method == "threshold")    return("—")
  val <- plist[[key]]
  if (is.null(val)) return("—")
  as.character(val)
}

param_label_map <- c(
  detection_method         = "Detection method",
  threshold_method         = "Threshold method",
  threshold_value          = "Threshold value",
  threshold_high           = "Threshold high",
  adaptive_block_size      = "Adaptive block size",
  clahe_clip_limit         = "CLAHE clip limit",
  denoise_h                = "Denoise h",
  sharpen_amount           = "Sharpen amount",
  morph_close_radius       = "Morph close radius (px)",
  morph_open_radius        = "Morph open radius (px)",
  min_feature_size         = "Min feature size (px²)",
  color_scale              = "Color scale",
  color_sigma              = "Color sigma",
  color_min_size           = "Color min size (px²)",
  color_morph_close_radius = "Color morph close radius (px)"
)

display_keys <- c(
  "detection_method",
  "threshold_method", "threshold_value", "threshold_high",
  "adaptive_block_size", "clahe_clip_limit",
  "denoise_h", "sharpen_amount",
  "morph_close_radius", "morph_open_radius", "min_feature_size",
  "color_scale", "color_sigma", "color_min_size", "color_morph_close_radius"
)

param_rows <- tibble(
  Parameter                = param_label_map[display_keys],
  `Moderate Threshold`     = map_chr(display_keys, ~ get_param(params_list[["moderate_threshold"]], .x)),
  `Optimised Threshold`    = map_chr(display_keys, ~ get_param(params_list[["opt_threshold"]], .x)),
  `Default Color Region`   = map_chr(display_keys, ~ get_param(params_list[["default_color"]], .x)),
  `Optimised Color Region` = map_chr(display_keys, ~ get_param(params_list[["opt_color"]], .x))
)

kable(param_rows, align = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c"))
```

---

## Grain Size Statistics

```{r stats-table}
stats_tbl |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2))) |>
  rename(
    Condition         = condition,
    `N (grains)`      = N,
    `Mean diam (µm)`  = mean_diam_um,
    `Median (µm)`     = median_diam_um,
    `SD (µm)`         = sd_diam_um,
    `CV (%)`          = cv_pct,
    `Min diam (µm)`   = min_diam_um,
    `Max diam (µm)`   = max_diam_um,
    `Mean area (µm²)` = mean_area_um2
  ) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 2))) |>
  pivot_longer(-Condition, names_to = "Statistic", values_to = "Value") |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = Condition, values_from = Value) |>
  kable(align = c("l", "r", "r", "r", "r"))
```

---

## Grain Size Distribution

### Histogram (density scale)

```{r histogram, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
x_lim <- max(grains_with_data$equivalent_diameter_um) * 1.05

ggplot(grains_with_data, aes(x = equivalent_diameter_um, fill = condition)) +
  geom_histogram(
    aes(y = after_stat(density)),
    binwidth = 5, boundary = 0,
    colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ condition, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, x_lim)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cond_colours) +
  labs(
    x = "Equivalent circle diameter (µm)", y = "Probability density",
    title = "Grain size distribution by condition (bin width 5 µm)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey92"))
```

### Density overlay

```{r density-overlay, fig.width=8, fig.height=4.5}
ggplot(grains_with_data, aes(x = equivalent_diameter_um, colour = condition, fill = condition)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.12, linewidth = 0.9) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, x_lim)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = cond_colours) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cond_colours) +
  labs(
    x = "Equivalent circle diameter (µm)", y = "Probability density",
    title = "Kernel density estimate — all conditions",
    colour = "Condition", fill = "Condition"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

---

## Log-Normal Distribution Fitting

Log-normal maximum-likelihood fits were computed with `fitdistrplus::fitdist()`.

### Fit parameters and goodness-of-fit

```{r fit-table}
fit_info |>
  left_join(conditions |> dplyr::select(label, method), by = c("condition" = "label")) |>
  rename(
    Condition = condition,
    Method    = method
  ) |>
  gt(rowname_col = "Condition") |>
  tab_header(title = "Log-normal fit parameters and information criteria") |>
  tab_spanner(label = "Log-normal parameters", columns = c(meanlog, sdlog)) |>
  tab_spanner(label = "Goodness of fit", columns = c(AIC, BIC)) |>
  cols_label(
    Method  = "Detection method",
    meanlog = "meanlog",
    sdlog   = "sdlog",
    AIC     = "AIC",
    BIC     = "BIC"
  ) |>
  tab_style(
    style     = cell_text(weight = "bold", color = "#00ACC1"),
    locations = cells_body(columns = AIC, rows = Condition == best_cond)
  ) |>
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "Highlighted cell = lowest AIC (best log-normal fit).",
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = AIC)
  ) |>
  tab_options(
    column_labels.font.weight       = "bold",
    stub.font.weight                = "bold",
    table.border.top.style          = "solid",
    column_labels.border.top.style  = "solid"
  )
```

### Fit overlay — lognormal density on histogram

```{r fit-overlay, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
x_seq <- seq(0.5, x_lim, length.out = 600)

curve_data <- map_dfr(conditions$label, function(lbl) {
  f <- fits[[lbl]]
  if (is.null(f)) return(NULL)
  p <- f$estimate
  tibble(
    x         = x_seq,
    y         = dlnorm(x_seq, p["meanlog"], p["sdlog"]),
    condition = factor(lbl, levels = conditions$label)
  )
})

grain_sub <- grains_with_data |>
  mutate(condition = factor(condition, levels = conditions$label))

ggplot() +
  geom_histogram(
    data = grain_sub,
    aes(x = equivalent_diameter_um, y = after_stat(density), fill = condition),
    binwidth = 5, boundary = 0,
    colour = "white", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = curve_data,
    aes(x = x, y = y, colour = condition),
    linewidth = 1.0
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ condition, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, x_lim)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cond_colours) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = cond_colours) +
  labs(
    x = "Equivalent circle diameter (µm)", y = "Probability density",
    title = "Histogram with log-normal fit overlay"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey92"))
```

### Q-Q plots (log-normal)

```{r qqplots, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))
for (lbl in conditions$label) {
  f <- fits[[lbl]]
  if (!is.null(f)) {
    qqcomp(
      list(f),
      legendtext = "log-normal",
      fitcol = cond_colours[lbl],
      main = lbl,
      xlab = "Theoretical quantiles",
      ylab = "Empirical quantiles"
    )
  }
}
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```
